fields.description:
## Why
Code review persists NOTHING to the shared store today: the reviewbot has only ephemeral /tmp gate snapshots
with no origin remote and no GitHub push credential (verified on the box). So there is no durable record and
no corpus to calibrate the new impact model from. (The RebarBotNava PAT push capability is already confirmed
working from the reviewbot container.)

## What
Emit a change-scoped `code_review` artifact keyed by (gerrit-change-id, revision), carrying the impact +
tier-tagged sub-answers + `norm_id`/`material_fingerprint` join keys, `relates_to` EVERY rebar ticket cited
by the change's commit trailers (many-to-one; session_log pattern - out of the dependency graph, excluded
from Jira sync, searchable). Concrete mechanisms:
- JOIN KEYS: `norm_id` and `material_fingerprint` are computed by the EXISTING plan-review sidecar
  machinery (reuse `plan_review.sidecar`/finder `norm_id` fingerprinting and the existing
  `material_fingerprint` function) applied to the code-review finding/diff â€” no new algorithm.
- CREDENTIAL: the reviewbot reads the PAT from its SSM-materialized `.env` (`REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT`) and
  configures a git credential helper that echoes it (`credential.helper='!f(){ echo username=x-access-token;
  echo password=$REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT; };f'`) so the token is never written to the remote URL or disk;
  `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always`. This is the exact mechanism validated by the on-box canary.
- STORE: a persistent tickets worktree materialized once (clone of the `tickets` branch) that rebar's engine
  writes to and auto-pushes to `origin` via the helper above.
- TRAILER RESOLUTION: parse all commits in the change for `rebar-ticket:` trailers; link the resolvable set;
  log unresolved/foreign refs (non-fatal).

## Scope
- Reviewbot infra (`docker-compose.yml`, `Dockerfile.reviewbot`, an SSM materialize step for the PAT + the
  persistent store) and the emission code (`code_review` artifact type/handling + the emit + trailer-link).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A `code_review` artifact type/handling exists: keyed by change+revision, kept OUT of the dependency
      graph, excluded from Jira sync, searchable (session_log-style).
- [ ] The reviewbot has a persistent tickets store whose `origin` authenticates via the PAT-echoing git
      credential helper sourced from SSM `.env` (never on disk / never in the remote URL); `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always`.
- [ ] `norm_id`/`material_fingerprint` reuse the existing rebar fingerprinting functions (no new algorithm).
- [ ] On a real review the artifact is written and lands on `origin/tickets`, linked `relates_to` to all
      trailer-cited tickets; unresolved refs logged, not fatal. No retention cap (deliberate).
